hero.subtitle: Autonomous AI agents that detect, patch, and verify vulnerabilities. Every fix is <highlight>grounded in your documentation</highlight> using RAG.
hero.subtitleBefore: Autonomous AI agents that detect, patch, and verify vulnerabilities. Every fix is

groundedIntelligence.title: Grounded security intelligence
groundedIntelligence.description: Each agent retrieves guidance from your security knowledge bases and use-case playbooks (e.g., secure login), so fixes stay consistent with your ground truth.

secureIngestion.title: Secure code ingestion
secureIngestion.description: Connect GitHub or GitLab and import repositories in minutes. VulnIQ maps your codebase and prepares it for review, remediation, testing, and reporting.

multiAgent.title: Multi-agent security workflow
multiAgent.description: A 4-agent pipeline: reviewer, implementer, tester, reporter. With per-agent specialized prompts and knowledge base for your stack and policies.

automatedValidation.title: Automated security validation
automatedValidation.description: After remediation, the Tester agent validates fixes with targeted security checks, confirming vulnerabilities are resolved without breaking behavior.

professionalReports.title: Professional security reports
professionalReports.description: The Reporter agent generates stakeholder-ready reports: findings, patches applied, evidence from knowledge bases, and test outcomes. Ready for audits.

importCode.title: Import your code
importCode.description: Connect GitHub or GitLab. Select a repository and VulnIQ maps your codebase automatically.

aiReview.title: AI reviews vulnerabilities
aiReview.description: The Reviewer agent scans your code against your knowledge base and security standards like CWE & OWASP.

agentsRemediate.title: Agents remediate & test
agentsRemediate.description: The Implementer applies fixes grounded in your docs. The Tester validates each patch passes security checks.

exportReport.title: Export verified report
exportReport.description: The Reporter compiles findings, patches, evidence, and test results into an audit-ready PDF report.

useCases.heading: Who benefits from VulnIQ?
useCases.description: From learners to teams, VulnIQ adapts to your workflow.

securityLearners.title: Security learners
securityLearners.description: Understand real vulnerability patterns and how to fix them with AI-guided remediation grounded in industry standards.

startups.title: Startups
startups.description: Ship secure code faster without a dedicated security team. VulnIQ catches and fixes vulnerabilities before they reach production.

universities.title: Universities
universities.description: Teach secure coding practices with hands-on AI-assisted vulnerability detection and remediation exercises.

openSource.title: Open source
openSource.description: Keep your open source project secure with automated vulnerability scanning and AI-powered fixes for public repositories.

teamsOfProgrammers.title: Teams of programmers
teamsOfProgrammers.description: Integrate VulnIQ into your development workflow for consistent, knowledge-base-grounded security remediation across your entire codebase.

comparison.heading: Why <brand>VulnIQ</brand>?
comparison.description: See how agentic RAG compares to traditional static analysis tools.

contextAwareAnalysis.label: Context-aware analysis
contextAwareAnalysis.traditional: Matches patterns without understanding surrounding code logic or project-specific context.
contextAwareAnalysis.vulniq: Agents retrieve relevant knowledge base entries and reason about code context before acting.

falsePositiveRate.label: False positive rate
falsePositiveRate.traditional: High — flags many non-issues, requiring manual triage to separate real vulnerabilities.
falsePositiveRate.vulniq: Reduced — grounded reasoning filters noise by cross-referencing your documentation and standards.

remediation.label: Remediation
remediation.traditional: None built-in — outputs a list of issues and leaves fixing entirely to the developer.
remediation.vulniq: Autonomous — the Implementer agent applies grounded fixes directly to your codebase.

knowledgeSources.label: Knowledge sources
knowledgeSources.traditional: Fixed rule sets and CVE databases that cannot be customized for your stack.
knowledgeSources.vulniq: Your own security knowledge bases, playbooks, CWE, OWASP, and stack-specific guidance.

fixVerification.label: Fix verification
fixVerification.traditional: None — no automated way to confirm a suggested fix actually resolves the vulnerability.
fixVerification.vulniq: Built-in — the Tester agent runs targeted security checks after each patch is applied.

reporting.label: Reporting
reporting.traditional: Raw scan output that needs manual formatting before it can be shared with stakeholders.
reporting.vulniq: Stakeholder-ready PDF reports with findings, patches, evidence citations, and test results.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about VulnIQ and how it can help secure your code.

q1.question: What is VulnIQ?
q1.answer: VulnIQ is an autonomous security remediation platform powered by a multi-agent RAG pipeline. It detects vulnerabilities in your code, applies grounded fixes using your own security knowledge bases, validates patches with targeted tests, and generates audit-ready reports — all without manual intervention.

q2.question: How does VulnIQ differ from traditional static analysis tools?
q2.answer: Traditional static analysis tools pattern-match against fixed rule sets and produce a list of issues with no remediation. VulnIQ uses a 4-agent pipeline (Reviewer, Implementer, Tester, Reporter) augmented with retrieval-augmented generation, so every fix is grounded in your documentation and security standards, verified by automated tests, and compiled into a stakeholder-ready report.

q3.question: Which programming languages does VulnIQ support?
q3.answer: VulnIQ currently focuses on web application languages and frameworks most commonly associated with OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities. Support is actively expanding — check the changelog for the latest language additions.

q4.question: Can VulnIQ integrate with my CI/CD pipeline?
q4.answer: CI/CD integration is on the roadmap. Currently VulnIQ connects directly to GitHub and GitLab repositories for on-demand scanning and remediation. Webhook-triggered and scheduled pipeline runs are planned for a future release.

q5.question: Which AI providers does VulnIQ use?
q5.answer: VulnIQ is model-agnostic by design. The platform is configured to work with leading LLM providers. Specific model selection and provider details are available in the documentation and may vary by deployment environment.

q6.question: Is there a free tier?
q6.answer: VulnIQ is currently in open beta as a research project. You can try the full demo without signing up. Account-based access is available — check the pricing page or contact us for details on current access tiers.

q7.question: How accurate is VulnIQ's remediation?
q7.answer: Accuracy depends on the quality of the knowledge bases provided and the complexity of the vulnerability. By grounding every fix in your documentation and validating with the Tester agent, VulnIQ significantly reduces false positives and unverified patches compared to unaided LLM output. Quantitative results are reported in the accompanying thesis paper.

metadata.title: About | VulnIQ
metadata.description: Learn about VulnIQ — a Master's thesis project investigating agentic RAG for automated vulnerability remediation.

about.intro: A Master's thesis project investigating the efficacy of LLM-based agents augmented with specialized security knowledge for vulnerability remediation.

problemStatement.heading: Problem statement
problemStatement.body: Large language models show significant promise for automated program repair, yet their application to security vulnerability remediation remains underexplored. Existing approaches rely on generic model capabilities without grounding fixes in authoritative security knowledge, leading to hallucinated patches, inconsistent remediation quality, and no automated verification of whether a fix actually resolves the vulnerability.

limitations.heading: Limitations of current approaches
limitations.body: Static analysis tools identify vulnerabilities but offer no remediation. LLM-based tools generate fixes without grounding in project-specific security policies or validated knowledge bases, producing a high rate of false positives and unverifiable patches that require significant manual review before they can be trusted.

methodology.heading: Proposed methodology
methodology.body1: VulnIQ implements a retrieval-augmented generation architecture composed of four specialized agents: a Reviewer that identifies vulnerabilities against curated knowledge bases, an Implementer that applies grounded fixes, a Tester that validates each patch with targeted security checks, and a Reporter that compiles audit-ready documentation.
methodology.body2: Each agent is equipped with a dedicated vector knowledge base containing CWE entries, OWASP guidelines, stack-specific secure coding patterns, and use-case playbooks. Retrieval ensures that every decision made by the pipeline is traceable to a specific knowledge source, enabling reproducible and auditable remediation.

keyCapabilities.heading: Key capabilities
items: Retrieval-augmented vulnerability remediation grounded in your security knowledge bases
End-to-end 4-agent pipeline with automated fix verification and test validation
Audit-ready PDF reporting with evidence citations and full patch traceability

contextualPrecision.title: Contextual precision
contextualPrecision.description: Evaluate whether RAG-augmented agents produce more contextually precise fixes than unaided LLMs by grounding remediation in project-specific knowledge bases.

automatedReasoning.title: Automated reasoning
automatedReasoning.description: Investigate the extent to which multi-agent reasoning can autonomously identify, prioritize, and resolve security vulnerabilities without human intervention.

fixVerification.title: Fix verification
fixVerification.description: Assess whether automated test generation and execution by the Tester agent provides reliable verification that applied patches resolve the targeted vulnerability.

knowledgeGrounding.title: Knowledge grounding
knowledgeGrounding.description: Measure the impact of specialized security knowledge bases on remediation quality, consistency, and traceability compared to generic model knowledge.

reportQuality.title: Report quality
reportQuality.description: Determine whether agent-generated security reports meet the standards required for stakeholder communication and compliance audit scenarios.

scalability.title: Scalability
scalability.description: Examine pipeline performance across repositories of varying size, language diversity, and vulnerability complexity to identify practical deployment boundaries.
